tasks.title: Initialize React Native Mobile Application
tasks.description: Set up React Native project structure with TypeScript, navigation, state management, and essential libraries for iOS and Android development.
tasks.details:
1. Initialize React Native project using latest CLI (0.73+): `npx react-native@latest init Foodie --template react-native-template-typescript`
2. Configure project structure: `/src` with folders `/components`, `/screens`, `/navigation`, `/services`, `/store`, `/utils`, `/types`, `/assets`
3. Install core dependencies:
   - Navigation: `@react-navigation/native @react-navigation/stack @react-navigation/bottom-tabs react-native-screens react-native-safe-area-context`
   - State: `@reduxjs/toolkit react-redux @tanstack/react-query`
   - UI: `react-native-paper` (Material Design)
   - Fonts: `react-native-google-fonts` for Poppins and Quicksand
4. Configure TypeScript strict mode in `tsconfig.json`
5. Setup ESLint + Prettier for code quality
6. Configure color palette constants in `/src/theme/colors.ts` with Fresh Avocado Green (#7CB342), Lime Zest (#9CCC65), Basil Leaf (#558B2F), Cream (#FFF8E1), etc.
7. Create typography system in `/src/theme/typography.ts` with Poppins/Quicksand font families and type scale
8. Setup React Navigation with bottom tab navigator (Wallet, Scan, Feed, Challenges, Profile)
9. Create base screen components for each tab
10. Configure Metro bundler for optimal build performance
tasks.testStrategy:
- Verify app launches on iOS simulator and Android emulator
- Test navigation between all bottom tabs
- Verify theme colors and fonts render correctly
- Check TypeScript compilation with no errors
- Test hot reload functionality

subtasks.title: Initialize React Native project with TypeScript template
subtasks.description: Create the React Native project using the latest CLI with TypeScript template and verify successful initialization on both iOS and Android platforms.
subtasks.details:
1. Run `npx react-native@latest init Foodie --template react-native-template-typescript` in the parent directory
2. Verify project structure created with standard RN folders: android/, ios/, App.tsx
3. Test app launch on iOS simulator: `cd Foodie && npx react-native run-ios`
4. Test app launch on Android emulator: `npx react-native run-android`
5. Verify Metro bundler starts successfully and hot reload works
6. Confirm TypeScript compilation works without errors
7. Document any setup issues in project README
subtasks.testStrategy:
- Run `npx react-native run-ios` and verify app launches showing default welcome screen
- Run `npx react-native run-android` and verify app launches on Android emulator
- Make a small change to App.tsx and verify hot reload updates the app
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify TypeScript compilation passes

subtasks.title: Setup project folder structure and install core dependencies
subtasks.description: Create the recommended folder structure within /src and install all required navigation, state management, and UI libraries.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `/src` directory structure:
   - `/src/components` - Reusable UI components
   - `/src/screens` - Screen components for navigation
   - `/src/navigation` - Navigation configuration
   - `/src/services` - API and external service integrations
   - `/src/store` - Redux store configuration and slices
   - `/src/utils` - Helper functions and utilities
   - `/src/types` - TypeScript type definitions
   - `/src/assets` - Images, fonts, and static files
   - `/src/theme` - Theme configuration (colors, typography)
2. Install navigation dependencies:
   `npm install @react-navigation/native @react-navigation/stack @react-navigation/bottom-tabs react-native-screens react-native-safe-area-context react-native-gesture-handler`
3. Install state management:
   `npm install @reduxjs/toolkit react-redux @tanstack/react-query`
4. Install UI library:
   `npm install react-native-paper react-native-vector-icons`
5. Install fonts:
   `npm install react-native-google-fonts` or setup custom font linking
6. Run `cd ios && pod install` to link iOS dependencies
7. Configure react-native-screens and gesture-handler in MainActivity.java and AppDelegate
subtasks.testStrategy:
- Verify all directories exist under /src with correct names
- Run `npm list` and confirm all packages installed successfully
- Check package.json shows correct versions for all dependencies
- Run `cd ios && pod install` and verify no errors
- Import a component from react-native-paper in App.tsx and verify no import errors

subtasks.title: Configure TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier and code quality tools
subtasks.description: Setup strict TypeScript configuration, ESLint rules, Prettier formatting, and ensure code quality standards are enforced.
subtasks.details:
1. Update `tsconfig.json` with strict mode settings:
   - Enable strict: true
   - Set strictNullChecks: true
   - Set noImplicitAny: true
   - Set skipLibCheck: true for performance
   - Configure path aliases: '@components/*', '@screens/*', '@services/*', etc.
2. Install ESLint and Prettier:
   `npm install --save-dev eslint @typescript-eslint/parser @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin eslint-plugin-react eslint-plugin-react-native prettier eslint-config-prettier eslint-plugin-prettier`
3. Create `.eslintrc.js` with React Native + TypeScript rules
4. Create `.prettierrc` with formatting rules:
   - semi: true
   - singleQuote: true
   - trailingComma: 'es5'
   - tabWidth: 2
   - printWidth: 100
5. Add scripts to package.json:
   - "lint": "eslint . --ext .js,.jsx,.ts,.tsx"
   - "format": "prettier --write \"src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx,json}\""
   - "type-check": "tsc --noEmit"
6. Configure VS Code settings.json for auto-format on save
7. Run lint and fix any existing issues
subtasks.testStrategy:
- Run `npm run lint` and verify it scans files without errors
- Run `npm run format` and verify it formats code
- Run `npm run type-check` and verify TypeScript compilation succeeds
- Intentionally add a type error and verify ESLint/TypeScript catches it
- Save a file in VS Code and verify Prettier auto-formats it

subtasks.title: Create theme system with color palette and typography configuration
subtasks.description: Implement the design system with color constants, typography definitions using Poppins and Quicksand fonts, and theme provider setup.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `/src/theme/colors.ts` with color palette:
   - Primary: Fresh Avocado Green (#7CB342)
   - Secondary: Lime Zest (#9CCC65)
   - Tertiary: Basil Leaf (#558B2F)
   - Background: Cream (#FFF8E1)
   - Surface: White (#FFFFFF)
   - Error: Red (#D32F2F)
   - Success: Green (#388E3C)
   - Text: Dark Gray (#212121), Medium Gray (#757575), Light Gray (#BDBDBD)
2. Setup custom fonts (Poppins, Quicksand):
   - Download font files from Google Fonts
   - Add .ttf files to /src/assets/fonts/
   - Create react-native.config.js to link fonts
   - Run `npx react-native-asset` or configure metro.config.js
3. Create `/src/theme/typography.ts`:
   - Define font families: { regular: 'Poppins-Regular', medium: 'Poppins-Medium', bold: 'Poppins-Bold', light: 'Quicksand-Light' }
   - Define type scale: h1 (32px), h2 (24px), h3 (20px), body (16px), caption (12px)
   - Include line heights and letter spacing
4. Create `/src/theme/index.ts` to export all theme values
5. Setup react-native-paper theme provider in App.tsx:
   - Customize default theme with Foodie colors
   - Wrap app in PaperProvider
6. Create example component using theme to verify it works
subtasks.testStrategy:
- Import colors from theme/colors and verify all color constants are accessible
- Render text with different font families and verify Poppins/Quicksand display correctly
- Create a Button with primary color and verify it uses Fresh Avocado Green
- Check typography scale values match design specifications
- Verify PaperProvider wraps the app and theme is accessible via useTheme hook

subtasks.title: Setup React Navigation with bottom tab navigator and base screen components
subtasks.description: Configure React Navigation with bottom tab navigator containing 5 tabs (Wallet, Scan, Feed, Challenges, Profile) and create placeholder screen components for each tab.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx`:
   - Setup NavigationContainer
   - Import createBottomTabNavigator
   - Configure 5 tabs: Wallet, Scan, Feed, Challenges, Profile
   - Add tab bar icons using react-native-vector-icons (MaterialCommunityIcons)
   - Customize tab bar styling with theme colors
   - Set active/inactive tint colors
2. Create placeholder screen components in `/src/screens/`:
   - WalletScreen.tsx - Display "Wallet" text centered
   - ScanScreen.tsx - Display "Scan" text centered
   - FeedScreen.tsx - Display "Feed" text centered
   - ChallengesScreen.tsx - Display "Challenges" text centered
   - ProfileScreen.tsx - Display "Profile" text centered
3. Configure TypeScript types for navigation:
   - Create `/src/types/navigation.ts` with RootTabParamList
   - Type-safe navigation props
4. Configure Metro bundler in `metro.config.js`:
   - Optimize bundle size with asset plugins
   - Configure source map generation
   - Enable Fast Refresh
5. Update App.tsx to use AppNavigator as root component
6. Test navigation between all tabs
subtasks.testStrategy:
- Launch app and verify bottom tab bar displays with 5 tabs
- Tap each tab and verify correct screen displays
- Verify tab icons display correctly and change color when active
- Verify tab bar uses theme colors (Fresh Avocado Green for active)
- Test Fast Refresh by changing screen text and verify it updates immediately
- Check TypeScript compilation passes with navigation types

subtasks.title: Install and Configure Google Fonts (Poppins & Quicksand)
subtasks.description: Set up Expo Font loader to properly load Poppins and Quicksand font families with all required weights for the typography system.
subtasks.details: The project already has @expo-google-fonts/poppins and @expo-google-fonts/quicksand installed, but fonts need to be properly loaded in App.tsx. Create a font loading wrapper component using expo-font's useFonts hook. Load Poppins (Regular-400, Medium-500, SemiBold-600, Bold-700) and Quicksand (Regular-400, Medium-500, SemiBold-600, Bold-700). Add a splash screen or loading indicator while fonts are loading. Update App.tsx to wrap the NavigationContainer with the font loader. Verify that typography.ts font family references work correctly after fonts are loaded. Test that all font weights render properly on both iOS and Android.
subtasks.testStrategy: Launch app on iOS simulator and Android emulator, verify fonts load without errors, check that different font weights (regular, medium, semibold, bold) display correctly in UI components, verify no font fallback warnings in console

subtasks.title: Create React Query Provider and Configure API Client
subtasks.description: Set up TanStack React Query provider with proper configuration for data fetching, caching, and error handling throughout the app.
subtasks.details: React Query is installed but not configured. Create /src/services/queryClient.ts to configure QueryClient with appropriate defaults: staleTime (5 minutes), cacheTime (10 minutes), retry logic (3 attempts with exponential backoff), refetchOnWindowFocus (false for mobile). Create /src/services/api.ts as base API client using fetch or axios with base URL configuration, request/response interceptors for auth tokens, error handling, and timeout settings. Wrap App content in QueryClientProvider in App.tsx. Create custom hooks folder /src/hooks with useQuery and useMutation wrappers for type safety. Set up React Query Devtools for development environment only.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify QueryClientProvider wraps navigation, test that React Query devtools appear in development mode, create a sample query hook and verify it properly fetches data with caching, test error handling with a failed API call, verify retry logic works as expected

subtasks.title: Configure Redux Store with Persistence and Dev Tools
subtasks.description: Enhance Redux store configuration with redux-persist for local state persistence and integrate Redux DevTools for debugging.
subtasks.details: The store is already initialized with @reduxjs/toolkit but needs persistence. Install redux-persist and @react-native-async-storage/async-storage. Create /src/store/persistConfig.ts to configure persistence with AsyncStorage, whitelist critical slices (user, wallet, settings), and set up migration strategy for version updates. Update /src/store/index.ts to wrap rootReducer with persistReducer. Create PersistGate wrapper in App.tsx to delay rendering until persisted state is retrieved. Set up flipper-plugin-redux-debugger or Reactotron for Redux debugging in development. Create initial slices: /src/store/slices/appSlice.ts for app-level state (theme, onboarding status). Ensure store rehydration works correctly on app restart.
subtasks.testStrategy: Install app, modify Redux state, close and reopen app to verify state persists, test that whitelisted slices persist while others don't, verify Redux DevTools connection in development, test that app doesn't crash during state rehydration

subtasks.title: Implement Base Screen Components with React Native Paper Theme
subtasks.description: Enhance existing screen components with proper React Native Paper theming, create reusable layout components, and implement consistent styling patterns.
subtasks.details: Existing screens (WalletScreen, ScanScreen, FeedScreen, ChallengesScreen, ProfileScreen) are basic placeholders. Create /src/theme/paperTheme.ts to configure React Native Paper theme using colors from colors.ts and fonts from typography.ts. Wrap App in PaperProvider with custom theme in App.tsx. Create base layout components: /src/components/layout/ScreenContainer.tsx (SafeAreaView wrapper with consistent padding), /src/components/layout/ScreenHeader.tsx (custom header component), /src/components/layout/LoadingScreen.tsx (loading spinner with brand colors). Update each screen to use ScreenContainer and add placeholder content with Paper components (Surface, Card, Text). Create /src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx component for use across screens. Ensure all screens follow consistent spacing and color scheme from theme.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to each tab and verify ScreenContainer renders with correct padding and safe areas, test that React Native Paper theme colors match the custom color palette, verify fonts render correctly in Paper Text components, test on both iOS notched devices and Android devices with different screen sizes

subtasks.title: Configure Metro Bundler and Optimize Build Performance
subtasks.description: Optimize Metro bundler configuration for faster development builds, configure path aliases, and set up production build optimizations.
subtasks.details: Metro config exists but needs optimization. Update metro.config.js to add watchFolders for monorepo support if needed, configure asset plugins for custom fonts and images, enable source maps for better debugging, add transformer configuration for inline requires to reduce bundle size. Configure babel.config.js to use module-resolver plugin with path aliases matching tsconfig.json paths (@components, @screens, @navigation, etc.). Add hermes optimizer flags for production builds in android/app/build.gradle and ios/Podfile. Create script commands in package.json: 'clean' (clear Metro cache), 'clean:android' (clean Android build), 'clean:ios' (clean iOS build), 'build:android' (production Android build), 'build:ios' (production iOS build). Test that path aliases work correctly throughout the app.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'npm run clean' and verify Metro cache clears, test that path aliases (@components/*, @screens/*, etc.) resolve correctly in imports, verify Hermes is enabled by checking app bundle, measure build time before and after optimization (aim for <5 seconds for incremental builds), test production builds on both iOS and Android

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into subtasks covering: (1) React Native project initialization with TypeScript, (2) folder structure setup and dependency installation, (3) TypeScript/ESLint/Prettier configuration, (4) theme system with colors and typography, (5) React Navigation setup with bottom tabs, (6-7) font loading (Poppins/Quicksand), (8) React Query provider configuration, (9) Redux store with persistence, (10) base screen components with Paper theme, (11) Metro bundler optimization, (12) missing dependencies installation, (13) complete folder structure, (14) font integration, (15) TypeScript validation, (16) final iOS/Android launch testing. Note: Project is already partially complete with navigation, theme, and basic screens implemented.

tasks.title: Setup Node.js Backend with Express and PostgreSQL
tasks.description: Initialize backend API server with Express.js, PostgreSQL database, Prisma ORM, Redis caching, and authentication infrastructure.
tasks.details:
1. Create `/backend` directory and initialize: `npm init -y`
2. Install dependencies:
   - Core: `express@5.x typescript @types/express @types/node ts-node nodemon`
   - Database: `@prisma/client prisma postgresql`
   - Auth: `jsonwebtoken bcryptjs @types/jsonwebtoken @types/bcryptjs`
   - Cache: `redis ioredis @types/ioredis`
   - Validation: `joi zod`
   - Environment: `dotenv`
   - CORS: `cors @types/cors`
3. Setup TypeScript configuration for backend
4. Create Express app structure: `/src/routes`, `/src/controllers`, `/src/middleware`, `/src/services`, `/src/utils`
5. Configure PostgreSQL connection (local dev + cloud production via DATABASE_URL)
6. Initialize Prisma: `npx prisma init`
7. Define Prisma schema with core entities: User, Restaurant, PointsTransaction, FoodiePoints, Order, SocialPost, Challenge, Event, Donation
8. Run first migration: `npx prisma migrate dev --name init`
9. Setup Redis connection for session storage and caching
10. Create authentication middleware using JWT (15min access token, 7day refresh token)
11. Implement rate limiting middleware (100 req/min per user)
12. Setup error handling middleware
13. Create health check endpoint `/api/v1/health`
14. Configure CORS for mobile app origins
tasks.testStrategy:
- Test database connection and Prisma queries
- Verify Redis connection and caching
- Test health check endpoint returns 200
- Test rate limiting blocks excessive requests
- Verify JWT token generation and validation
- Test CORS allows mobile app requests

subtasks.title: Initialize backend directory and install core dependencies
subtasks.description: Create /backend directory, initialize npm project, and install Express, TypeScript, Prisma, Redis, JWT, validation libraries, and development tools.
subtasks.details: Create `/backend` directory and run `npm init -y`. Install core dependencies: `express@5.x typescript @types/express @types/node ts-node nodemon`, database tools: `@prisma/client prisma`, authentication: `jsonwebtoken bcryptjs @types/jsonwebtoken @types/bcryptjs`, caching: `redis ioredis @types/ioredis`, validation: `joi zod`, environment: `dotenv`, and CORS: `cors @types/cors`. Verify all packages install without conflicts and are compatible with Node.js LTS version.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm list` to verify all dependencies are installed correctly. Check `package.json` contains all required dependencies with correct versions. Run `tsc --version` to confirm TypeScript installation.

subtasks.title: Configure TypeScript and create Express app folder structure
subtasks.description: Setup TypeScript configuration file and create organized directory structure for Express application with separation of concerns.
subtasks.details: Create `tsconfig.json` with strict mode, ES2020 target, and module resolution for Node.js. Configure `outDir` to `./dist`, `rootDir` to `./src`, and enable `esModuleInterop`. Create folder structure: `/src/routes` for API endpoints, `/src/controllers` for request handlers, `/src/middleware` for authentication/validation/error handling, `/src/services` for business logic, `/src/utils` for helper functions, and `/src/types` for TypeScript interfaces. Setup nodemon configuration for hot reloading during development.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify `tsconfig.json` compiles without errors using `tsc --noEmit`. Check all folders exist with correct naming. Create a test file in each directory to ensure TypeScript can resolve imports correctly.

subtasks.title: Configure PostgreSQL database connection and initialize Prisma
subtasks.description: Setup PostgreSQL database connection for local development and cloud production, then initialize Prisma ORM with proper configuration.
subtasks.details: Create `.env` file with `DATABASE_URL` environment variable pointing to PostgreSQL connection string (format: `postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/foodie_db`). Run `npx prisma init` to generate `prisma/schema.prisma` and configure datasource to use PostgreSQL. Set up separate DATABASE_URL values for development (local PostgreSQL) and production (cloud provider like Railway, Supabase, or Neon). Configure Prisma client generator and test database connectivity using `npx prisma db pull` or connection test query.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npx prisma db execute --stdin` with a simple SELECT query to verify database connection. Check `.env` file exists and contains valid DATABASE_URL. Verify `prisma/schema.prisma` generated with correct datasource configuration.

subtasks.title: Design Prisma schema with all core entities and relationships
subtasks.description: Define comprehensive Prisma schema including User, Restaurant, PointsTransaction, FoodiePoints, Order, SocialPost, Challenge, Event, and Donation models with proper relationships and constraints.
subtasks.details: In `prisma/schema.prisma`, define models: User (id, email, passwordHash, name, phone, profilePhoto, createdAt, updatedAt), Restaurant (id, name, location, cuisineTypes, priceRange, loyaltyProgramConfig), PointsTransaction (id, userId, restaurantId, amount, type enum, source, metadata JSON, createdAt), FoodiePoints (id, userId, restaurantId, balance, lastSyncedAt, expirationDate), Order (id, userId, restaurantId, items, totalAmount, pointsEarned, status, createdAt), SocialPost (id, userId, content, mediaUrls, likes, createdAt), Challenge (id, title, description, pointsReward, expiresAt), Event (id, restaurantId, title, date, attendees), Donation (id, userId, charityId, pointsUsed, amount, createdAt). Add proper relations (@relation), indexes (@@index), and unique constraints (@@unique).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npx prisma validate` to check schema syntax. Use `npx prisma format` to ensure proper formatting. Generate Prisma Client with `npx prisma generate` and verify no TypeScript errors in generated types.

subtasks.title: Execute first database migration to create all tables
subtasks.description: Run Prisma migration to create all database tables, indexes, and constraints based on the designed schema.
subtasks.details: Execute `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` to generate migration SQL files and apply them to the database. This creates all tables defined in schema (users, restaurants, points_transactions, foodie_points, orders, social_posts, challenges, events, donations) with proper columns, data types, foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Review generated migration file in `prisma/migrations` folder to ensure SQL is correct. Verify migration completes without errors and database schema matches Prisma schema exactly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check `prisma/migrations` folder contains migration files. Run `npx prisma migrate status` to verify migration applied successfully. Use database client (psql, pgAdmin, TablePlus) to inspect created tables and verify schema structure matches expectations.

subtasks.title: Setup Redis connection for session storage and caching
subtasks.description: Configure Redis client connection with ioredis for caching frequently accessed data and storing user sessions.
subtasks.details: Install and configure local Redis server or use cloud Redis provider (Redis Labs, Upstash, AWS ElastiCache). Create `/src/services/redis.service.ts` with ioredis client configuration using connection URL from `REDIS_URL` environment variable. Implement connection retry logic, error handling, and graceful shutdown. Create utility functions for common caching patterns: `getCache(key)`, `setCache(key, value, ttl)`, `deleteCache(key)`, and `flushCache()`. Setup Redis for session storage with TTL matching JWT refresh token expiration (7 days). Add Redis health check to verify connectivity on app startup.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test Redis connection with simple SET/GET operations. Verify cache TTL expires correctly after specified duration. Test connection retry logic by temporarily stopping Redis server. Check Redis health check returns connected status on app startup.

subtasks.title: Implement JWT authentication middleware with token management
subtasks.description: Create authentication middleware using JWT with 15-minute access tokens and 7-day refresh tokens, including token generation, validation, and refresh logic.
subtasks.details: Create `/src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts` with JWT verification logic. Generate access tokens (15min expiry) with payload: {userId, email, role}. Generate refresh tokens (7 day expiry) and store in Redis with userId mapping. Create `generateTokens(userId)` function returning {accessToken, refreshToken}. Implement `verifyAccessToken(token)` middleware that extracts token from Authorization header, verifies signature, checks expiration, and attaches user to request object. Integrate into signup route to hash passwords before storing in database. Integrate into login route to compare submitted password with stored hash. Never store plain-text passwords.

Create token utility: generateAccessToken(userId, email) with 15min expiry and generateRefreshToken(userId) with 7day expiry. Use strong JWT_SECRET and REFRESH_SECRET from environment variables (minimum 32 characters). Include user payload: { userId, email, iat, exp }. Return both tokens on successful login/signup. Store refresh token in database with userId, token hash, expiresAt, createdAt for revocation tracking.

On login/signup, set refresh token in cookie using res.cookie('refreshToken', token, options). Send access token in response body. Configure CORS to allow credentials from mobile app origin. On logout, clear cookie using res.clearCookie('refreshToken'). Never expose refresh token in response JSON.

subtasks.title: Build Token Refresh Logic

subtasks.details: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/refresh endpoint. Extract refresh token from HTTP-only cookie. Verify token signature and expiration using jwt.verify(). Check token exists in database and is not revoked. If valid, generate new access token with same user payload. Optionally rotate refresh token (generate new one and invalidate old). Return new access token in response body. If refresh token invalid/expired, return 401 and clear cookie. Implement token revocation on logout by marking database record as revoked.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test valid refresh token returns new access token, test expired refresh token returns 401, test revoked token returns 401, test token rotation invalidates old token, verify new access token works for protected routes

tasks.title: Build Unified Loyalty Wallet Backend & API
tasks.description: Implement backend logic for aggregating restaurant loyalty points, syncing balances, tracking transactions, and managing expiration alerts.
tasks.details:
1. Extend Prisma schema:
   - Restaurant model: id, name, location (lat/lng), cuisineTypes[], priceRange, loyaltyProgram {apiProvider, pointsFormula, oauthConfig}
   - PointsWallet model: userId, restaurantId, balance, lastSyncedAt, expirationDate
   - PointsTransaction model: userId, restaurantId, amount, type (earn/redeem/expire/gift), source, metadata, createdAt
2. Create `/api/v1/wallet` endpoints:
   - GET /wallet - Return unified wallet with all restaurants and balances
   - POST /wallet/connect - Connect new restaurant via OAuth or manual entry
   - POST /wallet/sync - Trigger sync for specific restaurant
   - GET /wallet/history - Get transaction history with filters
   - DELETE /wallet/disconnect/:restaurantId - Remove restaurant connection
3. Implement restaurant API integration service:
   - OAuth flow for supported chains (Starbucks, Chipotle, etc.)
   - Polling service to sync points every 4 hours (Bull queue + Redis)
   - Fallback to manual entry for unsupported restaurants
4. Create points normalization algorithm to display equivalent value
5. Implement expiration tracking:
   - Background job checks expiring points daily
   - Send push notifications at 30, 7, and 1 day before expiration
6. Add points calculation service:
   - Apply restaurant-specific formulas (e.g., $1 = 10 points)
   - Apply bonus multipliers (first visit 2x, challenges 1.5x)
7. Seed database with 20+ popular restaurant chains and their loyalty programs

subtasks.description: Build OCR text extraction logic to parse restaurant name, total amount, date/time, and line items from receipt images using Vision API.
subtasks.details: Send image to Google Vision API TEXT_DETECTION. Parse OCR response text blocks. Extract restaurant name using position heuristics (top 20% of receipt) and keyword matching. Extract total amount using regex patterns for currency ($XX.XX, total:, amount:, etc.). Extract date/time using date parsing libraries (moment/dayjs) with multiple format support. Optionally extract line items by parsing middle section. Return structured JSON with confidence scores for each field.

subtasks.title: Implement fuzzy restaurant name matching using fuse.js
subtasks.description: Build fuzzy matching algorithm to match extracted restaurant names against database despite typos and variations.
subtasks.details: Install fuse.js for fuzzy string matching. Fetch all restaurant names from database. Configure fuse.js with threshold 0.4, keys ['name', 'aliases']. Pass extracted restaurant name to fuzzy matcher. Return top 3 matches with scores. If top match score > 0.7, auto-select restaurant. If score 0.4-0.7, show user confirmation. If no matches, prompt user to select from dropdown. Cache restaurant list in Redis (refresh every 6 hours).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test fuzzy matching finds correct restaurant despite typos (e.g., 'McDonalds' matches 'McDonald's'). Verify matching handles case insensitivity. Test no false positives above threshold. Test cache improves performance.

subtasks.title: Implement duplicate receipt detection with image hashing
subtasks.description: Build duplicate detection system using perceptual image hashing to prevent re-scanning same receipt within 30 days.
subtasks.details: Install image hashing library (e.g., blockhash-js or sharp with phash). Generate perceptual hash of uploaded receipt image. Store hash in Receipt model with userId, timestamp, and receiptId. Query database for matching hashes (Hamming distance < 5) for same user within last 30 days. If duplicate found, return 409 Conflict with error message. If not duplicate, proceed with OCR processing. Add database index on (userId, imageHash, createdAt) for performance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test identical receipt images are detected as duplicates. Verify slightly rotated/cropped images still match. Test different receipts don't trigger false positives. Test 30-day window correctly filters old receipts.

Create Transaction record (userId, restaurantId, amount, pointsEarned, type: 'RECEIPT_SCAN', receiptId, createdAt). Update user's points balance atomically using database transaction. Handle concurrent updates with optimistic locking. Return points earned and new balance. Trigger points milestone notifications if applicable (from Task 7).

subtasks.description: Add geospatial distance calculation between user GPS coordinates and restaurant locations to power proximity-based ranking.
subtasks.details: Option 1 (PostGIS): Enable PostGIS extension in PostgreSQL, add geography column to Restaurant table, use ST_Distance(geography, geography) for accurate distance. Option 2 (Haversine): Implement Haversine formula in TypeScript: distance = 2 * R * asin(sqrt(sin²(Δlat/2) + cos(lat1)*cos(lat2)*sin²(Δlong/2))), R=6371km. Filter restaurants within maxDistance before scoring. Add distance field to response. Index geography column for performance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test distance calculation accuracy against known coordinates, verify PostGIS query performance with 10k+ restaurants, test Haversine fallback if PostGIS unavailable, validate distance filtering works correctly

subtasks.title: Implement collaborative filtering for 'users who liked X also liked Y' recommendations
subtasks.description: Build user-based collaborative filtering system to recommend restaurants based on similar users' preferences and behaviors.
subtasks.details: Create collaborativeFilteringService.ts. Track user interactions: saved restaurants, orders, high ratings (>4 stars) in UserInteraction table (userId, restaurantId, interactionType, weight, timestamp). Calculate user similarity using Jaccard similarity or cosine similarity on interaction vectors. Find top 10 similar users for each user. Aggregate their liked restaurants (weighted by similarity score), exclude already-interacted restaurants. Cache similarity calculations (update daily). Return top 5 collaborative recommendations to blend into feed (5-10% of results).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test similarity calculation with sample user data, verify recommendations exclude already-saved restaurants, test cold start (users with <3 interactions get popular items), validate cache invalidation

subtasks.title: Create /api/v1/feed/trending-dishes endpoint for popular menu items
subtasks.description: Build endpoint to surface trending dishes based on recent order frequency, post engagement, and rating velocity.
subtasks.details: Implement GET /api/v1/feed/trending-dishes endpoint. Calculate trending score: trendScore = (orderCount_last7days * 0.5) + (postEngagement_last7days * 0.3) + (ratingVelocity * 0.2). Query MenuItem with joins to Order and Post tables. Filter dishes with ≥5 orders in last 7 days. Return top 20 dishes with: dish details, restaurantInfo, trendScore, orderCount. Accept query params: lat/lng (proximity filter), cuisineType, dietaryRestrictions. Cache for 1 hour. Include 'Why Trending' reason (e.g., '127 orders this week').
subtasks.testStrategy: Test trending calculation with mock order data, verify dishes appear/disappear based on 7-day window, test proximity filtering works, validate cache expiration

subtasks.title: Implement local business spotlight logic for restaurants with <3 locations
subtasks.description: Add boosting mechanism to prioritize local independent restaurants over chains in the discovery feed.
subtasks.details: Add locationCount field to Restaurant model (default 1). Implement localBusinessBoost in ranking algorithm: if locationCount ≤ 3, multiply final score by 1.15. Add isLocalBusiness boolean to API response. Create admin endpoint PUT /api/v1/restaurants/:id/location-count for restaurant owners to update. Add 'Local Business' badge in UI. Optionally verify via business registration API. Track local business interaction rate for A/B testing boost effectiveness.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test boost applies only to restaurants with ≤3 locations, verify ranking changes with boost enabled/disabled, test admin endpoint updates locationCount, validate badge displays correctly

subtasks.title: Build mobile FeedScreen with infinite scroll using FlashList
subtasks.description: Create React Native discovery feed screen with performant infinite scrolling, pull-to-refresh, and optimized rendering for large datasets.
subtasks.details: Install @shopify/flash-list. Create FeedScreen.tsx in /screens. Implement useFeedData hook calling /api/v1/feed/restaurants with pagination. Use FlashList with estimatedItemSize={200}, onEndReached for pagination (fetch when 80% scrolled), onEndReachedThreshold={0.2}. Add pull-to-refresh with RefreshControl. Implement loading states (skeleton cards), empty state ('No restaurants found'), error handling. Memoize RestaurantCard with React.memo. Store feed data in Redux/Zustand with cache invalidation. Add floating 'Scroll to Top' button when scrolled >500px.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test smooth scrolling with 100+ items, verify pagination triggers correctly, test pull-to-refresh resets feed, measure frame drops with React DevTools Profiler, test offline behavior

subtasks.title: Create RestaurantCard component with quick actions (Save, Share, View Menu, Order)
subtasks.description: Build reusable card component displaying restaurant info with interactive quick actions and 'Why recommended' personalization text.
subtasks.details: Create RestaurantCard.tsx component accepting restaurant prop. Layout: Hero image (16:9 aspect), gradient overlay with name/cuisine, distance badge (top-right), rating stars + review count, price range ($-$$$$), 'Why recommended' text (e.g., 'Matches your love for Italian • 0.3 mi away'). Quick actions row: Save icon (heart, toggle saved state with animation), Share (native share sheet with deep link), View Menu (navigate to MenuScreen), Order Now (CTA button). Use Pressable with haptic feedback. Optimize images with react-native-fast-image. Handle long text truncation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test save toggle updates state and backend, verify share sheet opens with correct deep link, test View Menu navigation, test image loading performance, validate accessibility labels

subtasks.title: Implement filters (cuisine, price range, distance, dietary restrictions)
subtasks.description: Add filtering UI to discovery feed allowing users to refine results by cuisine type, price range, distance, and dietary needs.
subtasks.details: Create FilterModal.tsx with bottom sheet (react-native-bottom-sheet). Sections: Cuisine (multi-select chips: Italian, Mexican, Asian, etc.), Price Range (slider $-$$$$), Distance (slider 1-25 miles), Dietary (multi-select: Vegan, Vegetarian, Gluten-Free, Halal, Kosher). Store filter state in Redux/Zustand. Apply filters to /api/v1/feed/restaurants query params. Add 'Filters (3 active)' button in FeedScreen header. Show active filter chips below search with clear option. Persist filters to AsyncStorage. Implement 'Clear All' and 'Apply Filters' actions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test filter combinations update feed correctly, verify active filter count badge, test persistence across app restarts, validate slider range constraints, test 'Clear All' resets filters

subtasks.title: Add 'Not Interested' swipe left action to remove restaurants from feed
subtasks.description: Implement swipeable gesture to dismiss restaurants from feed and train recommendation algorithm based on user feedback.
subtasks.details: Install react-native-gesture-handler. Wrap RestaurantCard in Swipeable component. Configure leftAction: Red background with 'Not Interested' icon, threshold 50%. On swipe complete: Remove card from UI with fade animation, call POST /api/v1/feed/not-interested { restaurantId, userId } to track negative signal. Store dismissals in NotInterested table. Exclude dismissed restaurants from future feed queries (filter where restaurantId NOT IN dismissedIds). Add undo snackbar (5s timeout). Decrease ranking score for similar restaurants (same cuisine/price). Optionally ask reason (too expensive, too far, not my taste).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test swipe gesture triggers dismissal animation, verify restaurant removed from feed immediately, test backend stores dismissal, verify excluded from future feeds, test undo restores restaurant

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: (1) Prisma schema extension with MenuItem model, (2) /api/v1/feed/restaurants endpoint with pagination, (3) ranking algorithm implementation (user preferences 40%, proximity 25%, recency 15%, engagement 10%, diversity 10%), (4) proximity calculation using PostGIS or Haversine formula, (5) collaborative filtering implementation ('users who liked X also liked Y'), (6) /api/v1/feed/trending-dishes endpoint, (7) local business spotlight logic (restaurants <3 locations), (8) mobile FeedScreen with infinite scroll using FlashList, (9) RestaurantCard component with quick actions (Save, Share, View Menu, Order), (10) filters (cuisine, price, distance, dietary), (11) 'Not Interested' swipe action, (12) GPS location tracking with permission handling, (13) feed refresh on location change (>1 mile), (14) restaurant detail page, (15) cold start for new users, (16) feed caching with 10min TTL.

tasks.title: Develop Social Content Creation & Feed
tasks.description: Build social features allowing users to create food posts with photos, ratings, and captions, and view a feed of content from followed users and recommendations.
tasks.details:
1. Backend social infrastructure:
   - Extend Prisma schema: SocialPost model (userId, restaurantId, photos[], caption, rating, dishTags[], likes[], comments[], views, earnings, createdAt)
   - Comment model: postId, userId, text, createdAt
   - Follow model: followerId, followingId, createdAt
   - Create `/api/v1/posts` endpoints:
     * POST /posts - Create post (multipart for photos)
     * GET /posts/feed - Get personalized feed
     * POST /posts/:id/like - Like/unlike post
     * POST /posts/:id/comment - Add comment
     * DELETE /posts/:id - Delete own post
   - Implement feed algorithm:
     * 70% posts from followed users
     * 20% recommended posts from similar users
     * 10% trending posts nearby
     * Sort by reverse chronological with engagement boosts
   - Create `/api/v1/users/:id/follow` endpoint
   - Implement content moderation: Auto-flag profanity, hate speech using text analysis
2. Mobile post creation UI:
   - Create CreatePostScreen with photo selection (up to 5 photos)
   - Use `react-native-image-picker` for camera + gallery access
   - Implement image editor:
     * Filters: Vibrant, Warm, Crispy (food-optimized)
     * Adjustments: Brightness, contrast, saturation using `react-native-image-filter-kit`
     * Crop/rotate
   - Restaurant tagging: Autocomplete search with GPS suggestions
   - Rating selector (1-5 stars)
   - Caption input (500 char limit)
   - Dish tags (autocomplete from popular dishes)
   - Privacy settings: Public, Friends Only, Private
3. Mobile social feed UI:
   - Create FeedScreen with infinite scroll
   - PostCard component: Photos carousel, caption, restaurant tag, rating, like/comment/share buttons
   - Like animation (heart fill + pulse)
   - Comment modal with nested replies
   - User profile screen: Posts grid, followers/following count, badges
4. AWS S3 integration for photo uploads:
   - Backend generates presigned URLs for direct upload
   - Compress images before upload using `react-native-compressor`
   - Store originals + thumbnails (300x300, 800x800)
5. Implement follow system and suggested users
tasks.testStrategy:
- Test post creation uploads photos to S3
- Verify post appears in feed immediately
- Test image filters apply correctly
- Test restaurant tagging searches and attaches restaurant
- Test like/unlike increments counter
- Test comment posting and display
- Verify feed shows mix of followed + recommended posts
- Test follow/unfollow updates followers count
- Test content moderation flags inappropriate posts

subtasks.title: Extend Prisma schema with social models
subtasks.description: Create SocialPost, Comment, and Follow models in Prisma schema to support social content creation and user interactions.
subtasks.details: Add SocialPost model with fields: userId, restaurantId, photos[] (array of S3 URLs), caption (text), rating (1-5), dishTags[] (string array), likes[] (userId array), comments[] (relation), views (integer), earnings (decimal), createdAt. Add Comment model with: postId, userId, text, createdAt. Add Follow model with: followerId, followingId, createdAt. Run prisma migrate dev to apply schema changes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify migration creates tables correctly. Test model relations work. Confirm all fields have correct types and constraints.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/posts endpoint for post creation
subtasks.description: Create API endpoint to handle multipart form data for creating new social posts with photo uploads.
subtasks.details: Use multer or similar multipart parser to handle up to 5 photo uploads. Validate user authentication, restaurant exists, rating is 1-5, caption under 500 chars. Generate presigned S3 URLs for photo uploads (implemented in subtask 10). Store post in database with all metadata. Return created post object with ID.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with valid post data and photos. Verify photos upload to S3. Test validation errors for missing fields. Confirm post appears in database.

subtasks.title: Implement feed algorithm with personalized ranking
subtasks.description: Build feed algorithm that combines followed users, recommendations, and trending posts with engagement-based ranking.
subtasks.details: Query posts from followed users (70% weight), similar users based on restaurant/dish preferences (20% weight), and nearby trending posts (10% weight). Sort by reverse chronological order with engagement boosts: +2 hours per 10 likes, +1 hour per comment. Cache user following graph for performance. Implement pagination with cursor-based approach for infinite scroll.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test feed returns correct mix of content types. Verify engagement boosts reorder posts. Test pagination works correctly. Confirm followed users appear more frequently.

subtasks.title: Create GET /api/v1/posts/feed endpoint
subtasks.description: Expose personalized feed through REST API endpoint with pagination support.
subtasks.details: Accept query params: cursor (for pagination), limit (default 20, max 50). Use feed algorithm from subtask 3. Return array of post objects with user details, restaurant info, like/comment counts, and whether current user liked. Include nextCursor for pagination. Optimize with database indexes on createdAt, userId.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test feed loads for users with/without follows. Verify pagination works with cursor. Test performance with large datasets. Confirm posts include all required fields.

subtasks.title: Implement like/unlike and comment endpoints
subtasks.description: Create POST /api/v1/posts/:id/like, POST /api/v1/posts/:id/comment, and DELETE /api/v1/posts/:id endpoints.
subtasks.details: Like endpoint: Toggle userId in post.likes[] array, return updated like count. Comment endpoint: Create Comment record with postId, userId, text (validate non-empty, under 500 chars), return comment object. Delete endpoint: Verify user owns post, cascade delete comments, remove from S3 (subtask 10), delete post record. Add indexes for performance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test like increments/decrements count. Test comment creates and appears. Verify delete removes post and photos. Test auth prevents unauthorized actions.

subtasks.title: Create follow/unfollow endpoint
subtasks.description: Implement POST /api/v1/users/:id/follow endpoint to manage user follow relationships.
subtasks.details: Accept userId in path. Toggle Follow record between authenticated user (followerId) and target user (followingId). Prevent self-follows. Return updated follower/following counts. Invalidate feed cache when follow status changes. Add unique constraint on (followerId, followingId) pair.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test follow creates relationship. Test unfollow removes it. Verify can't follow self. Confirm counts update correctly. Test duplicate follows handled gracefully.

subtasks.title: Implement content moderation with text analysis
subtasks.description: Add automated content moderation to detect profanity and hate speech in captions and comments.
subtasks.details: Integrate text analysis library (e.g., bad-words npm package) or use AWS Comprehend for hate speech detection. Check caption on post creation and comment text on comment creation. Auto-flag posts/comments exceeding toxicity threshold. Store flagged status in database. Create admin review queue endpoint GET /api/v1/moderation/flagged. Allow manual approval/rejection.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test profanity triggers flag. Test hate speech detection works. Verify flagged content hidden from feed. Test admin review queue displays flagged items.

subtasks.title: Build CreatePostScreen with multi-photo selection
subtasks.description: Create React Native screen for post creation with camera and gallery access supporting up to 5 photos.
subtasks.details: Use react-native-image-picker for camera/gallery access. Implement photo selection UI showing selected count (X/5). Allow reordering photos via drag-and-drop. Show thumbnail previews. Add remove button per photo. Navigate to image editor (subtask 9) for each photo. Store selected photos in component state as URIs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test camera launches and captures photo. Test gallery selection works. Verify 5 photo limit enforced. Test photo reordering. Confirm remove button works.

subtasks.title: Implement image editor with filters and adjustments
subtasks.description: Build image editing interface with food-optimized filters and manual adjustments using react-native-image-filter-kit.
subtasks.details: Create ImageEditorScreen with photo carousel. Add filter presets: Vibrant (+saturation +sharpness), Warm (+color temperature), Crispy (+contrast +clarity). Implement adjustment sliders: brightness (-100 to +100), contrast (0-200%), saturation (0-200%). Add crop/rotate tools with common aspect ratios (1:1, 4:3, 16:9). Use react-native-image-filter-kit for filters. Save edited photo URI back to CreatePostScreen state.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test filters apply correctly. Test adjustments modify image. Verify crop/rotate works. Test edited image saves. Confirm performance acceptable on mid-range devices.

subtasks.title: Integrate AWS S3 with presigned URLs and compression
subtasks.description: Set up AWS S3 bucket, generate presigned URLs for direct uploads, and compress images before upload.
subtasks.details: Create S3 bucket with public read access for post photos. Backend generates presigned POST URLs (15min expiry) via AWS SDK. Use react-native-compressor to compress images to max 2MB before upload. Generate thumbnails server-side using Sharp: 300x300 for list view, 800x800 for detail view. Store original + 2 thumbnails. Return S3 URLs in post creation response.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test presigned URL generation works. Verify compression reduces file size. Test upload completes successfully. Confirm thumbnails generated. Test S3 URLs accessible publicly.

subtasks.title: Build FeedScreen with infinite scroll and PostCard component
subtasks.description: Create main social feed UI with infinite scrolling and rich post cards displaying photos, engagement, and interactions.
subtasks.details: Create FeedScreen using FlatList with onEndReached for infinite scroll. Fetch posts from GET /api/v1/posts/feed with pagination. Build PostCard component: user avatar/name, photo carousel (react-native-snap-carousel), caption, restaurant tag (tappable), star rating, like/comment/share buttons with counts. Implement optimistic UI updates for likes. Add pull-to-refresh. Cache images with react-native-fast-image.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test feed loads posts. Verify infinite scroll fetches more. Test pull-to-refresh works. Confirm photo carousel swipes. Test like button updates count. Verify performance smooth.

subtasks.title: Implement comments, user profiles, and follow system UI
subtasks.description: Build comment modal with nested replies, user profile screen, and follow/unfollow functionality.
subtasks.details: Create CommentModal: Fetch comments via GET /api/v1/posts/:id/comments, display with user avatars, support nesting (replies), comment input with POST. Add like animation: heart icon fills + scale pulse on tap using Animated API. Build UserProfileScreen: posts grid (3 columns), follower/following counts (tappable), follow/unfollow button, badges display. Implement restaurant tagging: autocomplete search using GET /api/v1/restaurants/search with GPS-based sorting, display recent/nearby suggestions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test comment modal opens and displays comments. Verify nested replies work. Test like animation plays. Confirm profile shows user posts. Test follow button updates status. Verify restaurant tagging searches correctly.

tasks.title: Implement In-App Ordering System with Stripe
tasks.description: Build complete food ordering flow including menu browsing, cart management, checkout with Stripe payment processing, order tracking, and automatic points earning. Frontend ordering flow is complete (MenuScreen, CheckoutScreen, OrderTrackingScreen, CartBottomSheet, MenuItemDetailModal) with Stripe PaymentSheet integration and cart state management. Backend order infrastructure is complete with Order/OrderItem models, Stripe service, and all order controller endpoints (create, confirm, get, history, status, cancel, track, menu). Points auto-crediting is implemented. Remaining work: POS integrations with email/SMS fallback, Stripe webhook endpoint, backend push notification service, and optional WebSocket support.
tasks.details:
✅ COMPLETED FRONTEND:
   - MenuScreen.tsx: Categorized menu display with SectionList, menu item cards with photos/prices/dietary tags, cart bar, integration with orderService (app/src/screens/MenuScreen.tsx)
   - MenuItemDetailModal.tsx: Bottom sheet modal with customizations, quantity selector, special instructions, Add to Cart action
   - CartBottomSheet.tsx: Cart items display with quantities, subtotal calculation, Checkout button
   - CheckoutScreen.tsx: Delivery/Pickup toggle, address input for delivery, tip input, points discount toggle (100pts=$1, max 50%), order summary with fee breakdown (subtotal, service 5%, delivery, tax 8.75%, tip), Stripe PaymentSheet integration (@stripe/stripe-react-native), Place Order flow (app/src/screens/CheckoutScreen.tsx)
   - OrderConfirmationScreen.tsx: Order number display, navigate to tracking
   - OrderTrackingScreen.tsx: Real-time polling (15s intervals), status progress bar, timeline visualization, restaurant contact info, estimated ready time (app/src/screens/OrderTrackingScreen.tsx)
   - Cart state management: Redux slice in app/src/store/slices/cartSlice.ts with addItem, removeItem, updateQuantity, setFulfillmentType, setDeliveryAddress, setTip, setPointsToUse, clearCart, calculateCartSubtotal
   - Order service: app/src/services/orderService.ts with fetchRestaurantMenu, createOrder, confirmOrder, trackOrder
   - Push notification client: FCM integration in app/src/services/notificationService.ts with message handling and navigation

✅ COMPLETED BACKEND:
   - Prisma schema: Order model (userId, restaurantId, status, fulfillmentType, subtotal, tax, deliveryFee, serviceFee, tip, total, pointsUsed, pointsEarned, deliveryAddress, specialInstructions, stripePaymentIntentId, estimatedReadyTime, completedAt, createdAt, updatedAt) with relations to User, Restaurant, OrderItem[] (backend/prisma/schema.prisma:211-243)
   - Prisma schema: OrderItem model (orderId, menuItemId, quantity, unitPrice, totalPrice, customizations, specialInstructions) (backend/prisma/schema.prisma:245-261)
   - Prisma enums: OrderStatus (PENDING, CONFIRMED, PREPARING, READY, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, COMPLETED, CANCELLED), FulfillmentType (PICKUP, DELIVERY, DINE_IN)
   - Stripe service (backend/src/services/stripe.service.ts): createPaymentIntent, getPaymentIntent, capturePaymentIntent, cancelPaymentIntent, getOrCreateCustomer, createEphemeralKey, createRefund with automatic_payment_methods enabled
   - Order controller (backend/src/controllers/order.controller.ts):
     * POST /api/v1/orders - Creates order, calculates fees (5% service, $2.99 delivery base, 8.75% tax), validates menu items, applies points discount (max 50%), creates Stripe PaymentIntent with ephemeral key for mobile, stores order with PENDING status
     * POST /api/v1/orders/:id/confirm - Confirms order after payment, updates status to CONFIRMED, deducts points used, creates points transaction, sets estimatedReadyTime (30min default)
     * GET /api/v1/orders/:id - Retrieves order details with items and restaurant info
     * GET /api/v1/orders/history - Paginated order history with filtering by status/restaurantId
     * PUT /api/v1/orders/:id/status - Updates order status (RESTAURANT_OWNER/ADMIN only), credits points on COMPLETED status (lines 571-606), creates audit trail
     * POST /api/v1/orders/:id/cancel - Cancels order, cancels Stripe PaymentIntent, refunds points used
     * GET /api/v1/orders/:id/track - Real-time tracking endpoint with status timeline and restaurant contact info
     * GET /api/v1/restaurants/:restaurantId/menu - Fetches menu items grouped by category
   - Order routes wired up in backend/src/routes/order.route.ts
   - Points auto-credit: Implemented in updateOrderStatus() when status changes to COMPLETED, upserts PointsWallet, creates EARN transaction (backend/src/controllers/order.controller.ts:571-606)

⚠️ PENDING BACKEND WORK:
1. Restaurant POS integration (NOT STARTED - no backend/src/services/pos-integration.service.ts found):
   - Update Prisma Restaurant model: Add posProvider (enum: NONE, SQUARE, TOAST, CLOVER), posAccessToken, posRefreshToken, posWebhookUrl, notificationEmail, notificationPhone
   - Update Prisma Order model: Add posOrderId field
   - Create pos-integration.service.ts with provider interface
   - Install Square SDK: `npm install square` in backend
   - Implement SquareProvider class: authenticate with restaurant.posAccessToken, call CreateOrder API
   - Install Toast SDK/HTTP client, implement Toast Orders API integration
   - Install Clover SDK, implement Clover REST API atomic orders endpoint
   - Implement email/SMS fallback: Install Twilio `npm install twilio`, SendGrid `npm install @sendgrid/mail`
   - Send SMS/email to restaurant.phone and restaurant.email when no POS integration configured
   - Integrate in confirmOrder controller: call POS integration after payment confirmation
   - Create admin endpoint PUT /api/v1/restaurants/:id/pos-settings for POS configuration
2. Stripe webhook endpoint (NOT STARTED - no backend/src/controllers/webhook.controller.ts found):
   - Create POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe endpoint
   - Validate webhook signature using stripe.service.constructWebhookEvent
   - Handle payment_intent.succeeded event to auto-confirm orders
   - Handle payment_intent.payment_failed event to mark order as failed
   - Configure Express to preserve raw body for webhook route
   - Configure webhook URL in Stripe dashboard
3. Backend push notification service (PARTIALLY COMPLETE - mobile client exists at app/src/services/notificationService.ts but no backend service):
   - Update User model: Add pushTokens (String[]) field
   - Install Firebase Admin SDK: `npm install firebase-admin`
   - Create backend/src/services/notification.service.ts: sendOrderNotification(userId, orderId, status, message)
   - Integrate in updateOrderStatus controller: send notifications for CONFIRMED, PREPARING, READY, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, DELIVERED
   - Create endpoint POST /api/v1/users/push-token to register device tokens
4. Optional WebSocket implementation (polling already works via OrderTrackingScreen.tsx:31-49):
   - Install socket.io: `npm install socket.io @types/socket.io`
   - Create WebSocket server in backend/src/index.ts
   - Implement authentication middleware for socket connections
   - Emit order status events when updateOrderStatus is called
   - Mobile app can subscribe to order-specific channels as alternative to polling
5. Testing:
   - Unit tests for order controller endpoints (validation, fee calculations, points logic)
   - Integration tests for Stripe payment flow (test mode keys)
   - Test points discount application and limits
   - Test order cancellation and refund flow
   - Test status transitions and points auto-credit idempotency

subtasks.details: 1. Update Prisma schema: Add to Restaurant model: posProvider (enum: NONE, SQUARE, TOAST, CLOVER), posAccessToken (String), posRefreshToken (String), posWebhookUrl (String), notificationEmail (String), notificationPhone (String). Add posOrderId (String) to Order model. Run prisma migrate dev. 2. Create backend/src/services/pos-integration.service.ts: Define interface POSProvider with sendOrder(order) method. Install Square SDK: npm install square. Implement SquareProvider class: authenticate with restaurant.posAccessToken, call ordersApi.createOrder() with mapped order data, return posOrderId. Install axios for HTTP clients. Implement ToastProvider class: use Toast Orders API (https://doc.toasttab.com/openapi/orders/operation/ordersPost/), authenticate with restaurant.posAccessToken, POST to /orders endpoint with Toast schema. Implement CloverProvider class: use Clover REST API (https://docs.clover.com/reference/ordercreateatomicorder), authenticate with bearer token, POST to /v3/merchants/{merchantId}/atomic_order/orders. Create factory function getProvider(restaurant) that returns appropriate provider or null. 3. Create fallback service: Install Twilio: npm install twilio for SMS. Install SendGrid: npm install @sendgrid/mail for email. Implement sendOrderNotification(order, restaurant) function. Format order details as readable text (items, quantities, total, customer info). Send SMS to restaurant.notificationPhone if exists. Send email to restaurant.notificationEmail if exists. 4. Integrate in order.controller.ts: After order confirmation (in confirmOrder), call POS integration. Try provider = getProvider(restaurant). If provider exists, call provider.sendOrder(order) and store posOrderId. If provider is null or fails, call sendOrderNotification(order, restaurant). Log all POS integration attempts for debugging. 5. Create admin endpoint for POS configuration: PUT /api/v1/restaurants/:id/pos-settings (RESTAURANT_OWNER only). Accept: posProvider, posAccessToken, posRefreshToken, notificationEmail, notificationPhone. Validate credentials by testing connection. Store encrypted tokens in database.

subtasks.title: Implement Stripe webhook endpoint for payment events
subtasks.description: Create webhook endpoint to receive and process Stripe payment events (payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed) for automatic order confirmation and failure handling.
subtasks.details: 1. Create backend/src/controllers/webhook.controller.ts: Implement POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe endpoint. Read raw body as Buffer (required for signature validation). Get stripe-signature header from request. Call stripe.service.constructWebhookEvent(rawBody, signature, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Handle event types: payment_intent.succeeded: Find order by stripePaymentIntentId, auto-confirm order (same logic as confirmOrder but without requiring user request), respond 200 OK. payment_intent.payment_failed: Find order, update status to CANCELLED, send notification to user, respond 200 OK. payment_intent.requires_action: Log for monitoring 3D Secure flows. Return 400 for signature validation failures. Return 200 for all handled events. Log all webhook events for debugging. 2. Configure Express to preserve raw body for webhook route: In backend/src/index.ts, add express.raw({type: 'application/json'}) middleware ONLY for /webhooks/* routes. Keep express.json() for other routes. 3. Configure Stripe webhook in dashboard: Go to Stripe Dashboard > Developers > Webhooks. Add endpoint URL: https://yourdomain.com/api/v1/webhooks/stripe. Select events: payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed, payment_intent.requires_action. Copy webhook signing secret to .env as STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Test with Stripe CLI: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/v1/webhooks/stripe.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test webhook endpoint validates Stripe signature correctly, verify payment_intent.succeeded auto-confirms order and deducts points, test payment_intent.payment_failed updates order to CANCELLED, test invalid signatures return 400, verify idempotency (duplicate webhook events don't cause issues), test webhook with Stripe CLI trigger commands, verify webhook works in production with real Stripe events

Update User model: Add pushTokens (String[]) field to store device tokens. Users can have multiple devices. Run prisma migrate dev. 2. Backend setup: Install FCM: npm install firebase-admin. Initialize Firebase Admin SDK with service account credentials. Create backend/src/services/notification.service.ts: sendOrderNotification(userId, orderId, status, message). Fetch user's push tokens from database. Send FCM notification to all tokens. Handle invalid/expired tokens (remove from user.pushTokens). 3. Create endpoint: POST /api/v1/users/push-token (authenticated) to register/update device tokens. Accept token (String) in body. Store in User.pushTokens array (append if not exists). 4. Integrate in order.controller.ts: In updateOrderStatus(), after updating status, call notificationService.sendOrderNotification(). Send notifications for: CONFIRMED ('Your order has been confirmed!'), PREPARING ('Your order is being prepared'), READY (fulfillmentType === PICKUP ? 'Your order is ready for pickup!' : 'Your order is ready!'), OUT_FOR_DELIVERY ('Your order is out for delivery'), DELIVERED ('Your order has been delivered'). Include orderId in notification data for deep linking. 5. Add notification preferences (optional): Allow users to opt out of certain notification types. Store preferences in User model: notificationPreferences (Json).

subtasks.title: Add optional WebSocket support for real-time order tracking

subtasks.details: OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENT (polling is already implemented and working at app/src/screens/OrderTrackingScreen.tsx:31-49): 1. Backend WebSocket setup: Install: npm install socket.io @types/socket.io. Create backend/src/services/websocket.service.ts. Initialize Socket.IO server in backend/src/index.ts alongside Express. Implement authentication middleware for socket connections (validate JWT token). 2. Order tracking room implementation: When user connects, validate JWT and get userId. Allow joining room: socket.join(`order:${orderId}`). Validate user owns the order before joining. 3. Emit events from order.controller.ts: Import websocket service. In updateOrderStatus(), after updating order, emit event: io.to(`order:${orderId}`).emit('order_status_updated', {orderId, status, estimatedReadyTime, updatedAt}). 4. Mobile WebSocket client: Install: npm install socket.io-client. Create app/src/services/websocketService.ts. Connect to WebSocket on OrderTrackingScreen mount. Authenticate with JWT token. Join order room: socket.emit('join_order', {orderId}). Listen for order_status_updated events and update local state. Disconnect on unmount. 5. Fallback strategy: Keep polling as fallback if WebSocket connection fails. Implement reconnection logic with exponential backoff. Monitor connection health with heartbeat pings. 6. Scaling considerations: For production, use Redis adapter for Socket.IO to support multiple server instances. Install: npm install @socket.io/redis-adapter redis.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test WebSocket connection authenticates successfully, verify joining order room requires order ownership, test status update events emit to correct room, verify mobile client receives events in real-time, test reconnection after connection drop, verify polling fallback works if WebSocket unavailable

tasks.title: Build Foodie Rewards Gamification System
tasks.description: Create challenge system including unique restaurant tracking, diverse dish tracking, cuisine exploration, restaurant-sponsored challenges, leaderboards, and achievement badges.
tasks.details:
1. Backend challenges infrastructure:
   - Extend Prisma schema:
     * Challenge model: type, title, description, requirements{count, specificItems[], deadline}, reward{foodiePoints, giftCard}, sponsorRestaurantId, participants[{userId, progress, completed}]
     * FoodiePoints model: userId, balance, totalEarned, totalRedeemed, lastActivity
     * Achievement model: userId, badgeType, earnedAt
   - Create `/api/v1/challenges` endpoints:
     * GET /challenges - Get active challenges
     * POST /challenges/:id/accept - Accept challenge
     * GET /challenges/:id/progress - Get progress
     * GET /challenges/leaderboard - Get leaderboard (local, national, friends)
   - Implement challenge tracking:
     * Unique restaurant: Track distinct restaurantIds per user, award points at milestones (1st: 10pts, 5th: 50pts bonus, 10th: 100pts bonus, etc.)
     * Diverse dish: Track distinct dish names, award points (1st: 5pts, 10th: 25pts bonus, etc.)
     * Cuisine exploration: Track distinct cuisine types, award points (1st: 20pts, 5th: 100pts bonus, etc.)
   - Restaurant-sponsored challenges:
     * Restaurants create challenges via web dashboard
     * Track user participation and completion
     * Auto-award points/gift cards on completion
   - Create background job to check challenge completions after each transaction
2. Mobile challenges UI:
   - Create ChallengesScreen with tabs: Active, Completed, Leaderboard
   - ChallengeCard: Title, description, progress bar, reward, Accept/Continue button
   - Progress indicators: "7/10 unique restaurants" with visual progress
   - Achievement badges showcase on profile
   - Leaderboard: Rank, username, photo, points, badges
   - Challenge completion animation: Badge scales up + confetti
3. Implement gift card redemption (Tango Card API):
   - Create `/api/v1/rewards/redeem` endpoint
   - Integrate Tango Card API for digital gift cards
   - Redemption catalog: Restaurant chains, retailers (Amazon, Target), Visa prepaid
   - Pricing: 1,000pts = $10, 2,500pts = $25, 5,000pts = $50
   - Deliver gift cards via email + in-app wallet
4. Add Foodie Points separate wallet section
5. Implement points expiration: 12 months of inactivity

tasks.title: Implement Value Dashboard and Budget Tracking
tasks.description: Build value analysis dashboard showing best value restaurants, cost-per-meal insights, savings summary, and budget tracking to help users make informed dining decisions.
tasks.details:
1. Backend value calculations:
   - Create `/api/v1/value` endpoints:
     * GET /value/best-value - Get top 5 best value restaurants
     * GET /value/savings - Get monthly and lifetime savings
     * GET /value/cost-per-meal - Get cost analysis by restaurant
     * PUT /value/budget - Set monthly budget
     * GET /value/budget - Get budget tracking
   - Implement value score algorithm:
     * valueScore = (pointsEarned / cost) + (availableDiscounts / cost) + qualityRating
     * Weight factors: High points (8x), Low cost ($$), Great rating (4.5★)
   - Calculate cost per meal:
     * Average: (subtotal + deliveryFee + tax + tip - pointsDiscount) / numOrders
     * Group by restaurant
   - Track savings:
     * Monthly savings = total points discounts + promotional discounts
     * Lifetime savings = sum of all savings
   - Implement budget tracking:
     * Track spending against monthly budget
     * Alert when 80% reached
     * Breakdown by category (breakfast, lunch, dinner)
2. Mobile value dashboard UI:
   - Create ValueScreen (new tab in bottom nav)
   - BestValueCard: Restaurant logo, name, value score (9.2/10), breakdown text
   - SavingsSummary: This month, lifetime, graph of monthly trend
   - BudgetTracker: Progress bar, spent/remaining, category breakdown
   - CostPerMealChart: Bar chart or list of restaurants ranked by avg cost
   - DecisionHelper: "Where Should I Eat?" button opens questionnaire
     * Questions: Craving (dropdown), Price range (slider), Distance (slider), Priority (value/speed/quality)
     * Show top 3 recommendations with reasoning
   - ValueAlerts: Real-time notifications for bonus points, expiring points, flash sales
3. Implement daily value digest email:
   - Create cron job to send email at 10 AM daily

tasks.description: Improve receipt scan accuracy to 90%+ using Google Vision API OCR, implement perceptual hashing for duplicate detection, add restaurant fuzzy matching, and receipt confirmation UI as per PRD section 3.2.1
tasks.details: Integrate Google Cloud Vision API for OCR processing with receipt-specific optimizations: extract restaurant name, transaction date/time, total amount, tax amount, and line items (optional for dish tracking).
